title: mallotus surculosus
intro: Mallotus surculosus, also known as the spurge of the forest, is a type of plant that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mallotus surculosus include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, with the leaves and roots being the most commonly used parts.
